description:
Enable this to use virtually-mapped kernel stacks with guard pages.
This configuration is available from kernel 4.9.

The configuration that was used to build kernel is available at <tt>/boot/config-*</tt>.
    To check the configuration value for <tt>CONFIG_VMAP_STACK</tt>, run the following command:
    <tt>grep CONFIG_VMAP_STACK /boot/config-*</tt>
    
    For each kernel installed, a line with value "y" should be returned.
rationale: This causes kernel stack overflows to be caught immediately rather than causing difficult-to-diagnose corruption.
ocil_clause: the kernel was not built with the required value
ocil:
To determine the config value the kernel was built with, run the following command: 
$ grep CONFIG_VMAP_STACK /boot/config.*
For each kernel installed, a line with value "y" should be returned.

warnings.general: There is no remediation for this besides re-compiling the kernel with the appropriate value for the config.

title: User a virtually-mapped stack
